What do you do? I was taught one early on and used it while I know there's books of 365 and 152 million. You don't need 365. You know, one favorite day of the week. But at the end of the day, this makes sense to me, Dave. How about what do you think? That's what I use that. Yeah, what do you think? I'm Marvel Boxing Man, guys. So I'm an AB or an ABC guy. So you do alternative choice. I will almost always present alternatives. So where we are, what you want to accomplish, here's sort of the Rolls Royce of this. Everything at once, the best speed, all that. Or you could do it in these three stages. So I'm an ABC guy. I like the alternative of choice rather than a yes, no. I don't mind the trade down. So some people won't do it because they're afraid of the trade down. If I give them three choices, they'll take the low. I'm not really worried about that. So I will almost always use alternative choice and alternative choice language. My sort of urgency language is, even if it's not ask, I will say, I'm sure you're going to want to know when this can get done. So use your calendar to move on. My schedule will tell you. If you said yes yesterday, here's what the timeline is going to look like. And I am almost always selling, by the way, in an environment where they want it faster than I can do it. So that's there as a negative to be overcome. But I will use that to create urgency. I will try to be in an environment where they see that is real. So what would your exact language be, though?
